text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** is running hot with **$73.42 top scale** and 23 calls hunting 117 hands. Mid City and TSG are loading the board across various locations, while ESI's Cologix Data Center gig locks in **double-time OT** on a M-F 40-hour floor. If you want union wages at their peak, this is the play right now.

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** has **$54.75 scale** with 43 calls and strong depth. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center jobs pay **10% over scale with rollups** on 5-12 hour days—serious coin for serious hours. Industrial Electric adds rotating shifts on the Lilly LP1 new construction side. Lebanon is booming.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** shows 41 calls chasing 169 hands, all centered on Port Washington Data Center. Multiple contractors (MJ Electric, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein) are reposting the same gig at **JW wage plus $10/hr base**—that's premium money for data center work in Wisconsin.

**LU-1105 Newark, OH** has the biggest board at 33 calls for 283 hands, but hit the brakes: posted scale is **$0.00**, which means union negotiation or prevailing wage. Superior Group and Mid-City dominate with 4-10 schedules and heavy Saturday availability—good work if you don't mind the grind.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** offers **$44.80 scale** plus **$100/day non-taxed per diem** on Polaris Forge Data Center work (75 hands needed across three Parsons postings). Shift differential runs **31.4%**—northern grit pays.

Data centers are still the bread-and-butter chase nationally. Book depth remains strong across the board.
